title: Playbook - Qualys VMDR Reporting And Metrics Extraction
applies_to: Building engine workflows that generate VMDR reports by asset, asset group, tag, month, and quarter
prerequisites: User identifies the tenant platform from https://www.qualys.com/platform-identification
Project config stores both the classic Qualys API server URL and the API gateway URL for the tenant
Credentials or tokens are available for both the classic VM API family and gateway asset inventory APIs as needed
Report request states the asset scope and whether the output is snapshot, period activity, or trend
required_permissions: Classic VM visibility to target hosts and detections
KnowledgeBase download permission when KB enrichment is required
GAV or CSAM module plus App API Enabled and view access permissions for Asset Inventory routes
request_sequence: Resolve tenant platform and derive the classic API server URL and gateway URL from the official Qualys platform-identification reference.

Resolve the reporting scope. For a single asset, resolve by assetId, hostId, IP, or hostname. For a group or tag report, resolve the asset set first, preferably through Asset Inventory search filters and tag metadata.
Pull or refresh the asset inventory dimension from POST /rest/2.0/search/am/asset using includeFields to retain only the fields needed for reporting and grouping.
Pull host detections from /api/2.0/fo/asset/host/vm/detection/?action=list for the scoped assets. Use show_asset_id=1 and show_qds=1. Add tag parameters when the tenant scoping model supports them.

For change-window reports, use detection_updated_since/before or vm_processed_after/before. For current-state reports, use the current detection set without a date window unless the user asks otherwise.
Collect the distinct QIDs from the detection result and enrich them through /api/2.0/fo/knowledge_base/vuln/ with details=All, scoped to the referenced ids when possible.
Normalize to one detection-instance row per asset with KB title, severity metadata, CVEs, asset name, network identifiers, and tag context.
Aggregate into the requested output shape: by asset, by tag, by severity, by title, by month, or by quarter.
State the timestamp basis explicitly for any previous month or previous quarter report.
common_report_recipes: Current vulnerabilities for one asset: scope to one asset, current detection rows only, group by status or severity as requested.
Current vulnerabilities for a tag: resolve tag membership in Asset Inventory, fetch detections for the resulting asset set, group by severity, title, or asset.
Previous month activity: choose one timestamp basis such as FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME for newly found or LAST_FIXED_DATETIME for remediated items, then aggregate within the previous calendar month.
Previous quarter summary: bucket rows by calendar quarter using the chosen timestamp basis, then produce counts by severity, status transition, or title.
Top vulnerabilities for a scoped population: aggregate current or period rows by QID and KB TITLE, then sort by count descending.

confirmed_facts.fact: The Sumo Logic Qualys VMDR integration documents the same three-source model used here: vulnerability detections from /api/2.0/fo/asset/host/vm/detection/, KnowledgeBase data from /api/2.0/fo/knowledge_base/vuln/, and asset data from /rest/2.0/search/am/asset/.
The Host Detection List API returns latest vulnerability data for hosts and supports QDS plus filterable date windows for changed or processed detections.
The Asset Inventory API documents includeFields or excludeFields for payload reduction and exposes hostId, assetId, names, addresses, OS, and tags in asset payloads.

implementation_notes: When the user asks for 'all vulnerabilities as a point in time', interpret that as a current-state snapshot unless stored historical snapshots exist. For a past point in time, use persisted extracts rather than assuming live APIs can recreate old dashboard state exactly.

When the user asks for 'previous month' or 'previous quarter' without clarifying the timestamp basis, default to a clearly labeled basis such as LAST_UPDATE_DATETIME for activity or FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME for newly discovered findings, and surface that choice in the report header.
Prefer deriving asset groups from asset inventory filters and tags instead of trying to reconstruct groups only from classic VM host fields.
Use KB TITLE as the stable display name for vulnerability-name reports and keep QID alongside it to avoid collisions or renamed titles.
Keep the normalized output durable enough to support repeated monthly or quarterly rollups without reinterpreting raw XML every time.
common_failures: Wrong pod or wrong URL family, causing classic VM calls and gateway calls to fail in different ways.
Missing KB permission, which leaves QIDs without titles, CVEs, or patchability metadata.
Omitting show_asset_id=1, which makes classic-to-gateway joins harder than necessary.
Building a previous-month report without stating which timestamp defines inclusion.
Treating current live detections as if they were a historical snapshot for a past date.
